# Comparing fibroblasts in different conditions

## Plating fibroblasts in a u-bottom low attachment plate

Explanations:

1 

 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 
 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Explanations:

- Centrifuged = The plate was centrifuged after the content was added
 > to the wells, and the cells were not resuspended in the media/gel
 > solution. In addition, (1) the plate was kept cold so that the
 > Cultrex droplets did not start solidifying until after the
 > centrifugation, and (2) fibrinogen was added carefully to the
 > wells after centrifugation so that the ome-gels/fibrin matrices
 > didn't either solidify before centrifugation.

- Cell suspension = cells in media

- Cells in omentum = omentum diluted in media

- Cells in "normal" ome-gel = ome-gel prepared as previously

- Cells in ome-gel, 1.06 mg/ml fibrin = ome-gel, but volume of
 > fibrinogen, aprotinin, and thrombin was increased by x2.12

- Cells in ome-gel, 2.5 mg/ml fibrin = ome-gel but volume of
 > fibrinogen, aprotinin, and thrombin was increased by x5

- Cells in 1.06 mg/ml Cultrex = Cultrex diluted in media (NOTE!
 > Cultrex was previously diluted in DPBS)

- Cells in 2.5 mg/ml Cultrex = Cultrex diluted in media

- Cells in 7.5 mg/ml Cultrex = Cultrex diluted in media

- Fibrin matrix = same concentrations of fibrinogen, aprotinin, and
 > thrombin as in the original ome-gel, but diluted in media (no
 > omentum).

trex = Cultrex diluted in media

- Fibrin matrix = same concentrations of fibrinogen, aprotinin, and
 > thrombin as in the original ome-gel, but diluted in media (no
 > omentum).

Preparations:

1. Bring the ice to the CC room

2. Thaw Cultrex, Omentum, aprotinin and thrombin on ice

3. Prepare media: DMEM/F12+++ +10% FBS

Calculation:
[[https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1WyUYeD-VWNPZzBFqtTwkWyDttPLFj7Ha2QnlgFRra8E/edit?usp=drive_link]{.underline}](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1WyUYeD-VWNPZzBFqtTwkWyDttPLFj7Ha2QnlgFRra8E/edit?usp=drive_link)

1. Detach and prepare cells

 - Remove the media and wash cells twice with PBS

 - Detach the cells with 2 ml warmed (+37°C) trypsin for 2 min in
 > the cell incubator.

 - Add PBS, pipette the solution around the plate, and transfer to
 > a falcon tube. Ensure all cells are detached.

 - Centrifuge 300 g for 5 min, discard the supernatant, and
 > resuspend in 1 ml of media.

2. Count the cells.

3. Prepare cell solutions of acquired concentration. The final volume
 > of each cell suspension should be at least 1070 µl.

 - Prepare 1873 of 2 E6 cells/ml solution.

 - To prepare 1 E6 cells/ml solution, take 803 µl of 2 E6 solution
 > and add another 803 µl of media.

 - To prepare a 0.5 E6 cells/ml solution, take 535 µl of 1 E6
 > solution and add another 535µl of media.

```{=html}
<!-- -->
```
4. Prepare Cultex solutions

r 803 µl of media.

 - To prepare a 0.5 E6 cells/ml solution, take 535 µl of 1 E6
 > solution and add another 535µl of media.

```{=html}
<!-- -->
```
4. Prepare Cultex solutions

 - Calculate the needed volumes to prepare Cultrex solutions, and
 > dilute the Cultrex stock into media.

5. Prepare ome-gel solutions

 - Combine the required amount of media, omentum-gel, thrombin, and
 > aprotinin.

 - Do not add fibrinogen! In the meantime, warm up fibrinogen in
 > +37°C water bath.

6. Prepare omentum+media solution

 - Combine the required amount of media and omentum-stock.

7. Prepare fibrin matrix solution.

 - Combine the required amount of media, thrombin, and aprotinin.

 - Do not add fibrinogen! In the meantime, warm up fibrinogen in
 > +37°C water bath.

 - 

Prepare aliquots of cells

1. To prepare cells for the Cultrex droplets, make 3 aliquots (2x 30 µl
 > each) of each of the 3 different cell solutions.

2. To prepare cells for the ome-gel droplets, make 3 aliquots (2x 30 µl
 > each) of each of the 3 different cell solutions.

3. To prepare cells for the fibrin droplets, make 1 aliquot (30 µl) of
 > each of the 3 different cell solutions.

4. To prepare the omentum+media wells, make 1 aliquot (110 µl) of the 3
 > different cell solutions.

5. Centrifuge the aliquots at 300 g 5 min +4°C, and remove the
 > supernatant. Keep the cells on ice.

+media wells, make 1 aliquot (110 µl) of the 3
 > different cell solutions.

5. Centrifuge the aliquots at 300 g 5 min +4°C, and remove the
 > supernatant. Keep the cells on ice.

Plating the cells that will be centrifuged

1. Prepare Cultrex droplets

 a. Add 60 µL of diluted 10 % Cultrex solution to one with each cell
 > concentration and mix gently.

 b. Pipette one 20 µL droplet into the corresponding wells from each
 > cell suspension. Keep the remaining cell suspension with
 > Cultrex on ice.

 c. Repeat with 2.5 mg/ml and 7.5 mg/ml Cultrex solution. Pipette
 > one 20 µL droplet into the corresponding wells from each cell
 > suspension. Keep the remaining cell suspension with Cultrex on
 > ice.

2. Prepare omentum droplets

 a. Add 60 µL of 2.5 mg/ml ome-gel solution w/o fibrinogen solution
 > on top of the cells.

 b. Pipette one 20 µL droplet into the corresponding wells from each
 > cell suspension. Keep the remaining cell suspension on ice.

 c. Repeat with 2.12x and 5x ome-solutions. Pipette one 20 µL
 > droplet into the corresponding wells from each cell
 > suspension.

3. Prepare fibrin droplets

 a. Add 30 µl of fibrin solution without fibrinogen on top of the
 > cells. Pipette one 20 µL droplet into the corresponding wells
 > from each cell suspension. Keep the remaining cell suspension
 > on ice.

4. Plate cells in media.

 a.

p of the
 > cells. Pipette one 20 µL droplet into the corresponding wells
 > from each cell suspension. Keep the remaining cell suspension
 > on ice.

4. Plate cells in media.

 a. Take one 100 µl aliquot of each cell suspension and plate the
 > cells.

5. Plate cells in omentum + media solution.

 a. Add 220 µl of omentum-media solution on top of the cells, mix,
 > and plate 100 µl of the solution.

```{=html}
<!-- -->
```
6. Centrifuge 200 x g for 3 min +4°C.

7. Plate the remaining wells with corresponding cell density and
 > condition.

8. Incubate 1 hour at +37C.

9. During the 1 hour incubation, warm up the media.

10. Add 200 µl media per well with Cultrex, ome-gel or fibrin matrix.

### Fixation of the cells

Based on "3D IF for organoids" protocol by Anna Nylund
([[https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Wl6zjriFb2M8FejlJqw3isF6_JaoZNLhcmScBFtdouU/edit?usp=sharing]{.underline}](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Wl6zjriFb2M8FejlJqw3isF6_JaoZNLhcmScBFtdouU/edit?usp=sharing)).

1. Warm up 4% PFA + 0.2 % glutaraldehyde\* in PBS to 37[°C]{.mark}.
 > Carefully remove the medium without disturbing the droplet. Add
 > 200 [μ]{.mark}l per well, and fix at RT for 20 minutes.

 a. All together 42 wells, prepare 8,4 ml

 b. 34 µl of glutaraldehyde in 8,366 ml 4% PFA

2. Remove the fixative and wash the samples with PBS 3 times. If

at RT for 20 minutes.

 a. All together 42 wells, prepare 8,4 ml

 b. 34 µl of glutaraldehyde in 8,366 ml 4% PFA

2. Remove the fixative and wash the samples with PBS 3 times. If
 > storing, remember to leave a large volume of PBS per well.

## Plating fibroblasts in the lid of a flat bottom 96-well plate

 -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. While preparing the Cultrex gel, ome-gel, and fibrin gel solutions,
 > prepare enough for these droplets as well.

2. Prepare aliquots of cell suspension as described above.

 a. Make 7 aliquots (30 µl) of each of the different cell densities,
 > centrifuge 300 g 5 min +4°C, and remove the supernatant. Keep
 > the cells on ice.

3. To prepare the droplets, add 30 µl of the corresponding
 > Cultrex/ome-gel/fibrin solution to the cells.

4. Cultrex droplets: mix well and pipette one 20 µL droplet into the

3. To prepare the droplets, add 30 µl of the corresponding
 > Cultrex/ome-gel/fibrin solution to the cells.

4. Cultrex droplets: mix well and pipette one 20 µL droplet into the
 > corresponding place in the lid.

5. Ome-gel and fibrin gel droplets: Mix well, add 0,6 µl of warmed
 > fibrinogen, mix again, and pipette one 20 µL droplet into the
 > corresponding place in the lid.

6. Incubated for 1 hour. Meanwhile, warm up the media.

7. Add 260 µl media to the wells so that the well is full and the
 > droplet is surrounded with media.
